Plantago lanceolata, commonly known as Plantain, is a type of herbaceous perennial plant native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. Plantain has been traditionally used for its health benefits, including the treatment of gastrointestinal issues, wound healing, and reduction of inflammation. The bioactive constituents of Plantain include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and iridoid glycosides, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Plantain is commonly prepared as a tea, tincture, or dried herb, and is often used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antidiarrheal effects.
